## 72. Plik: `mobile\src\constants\transcripts.json`

```json
{
  "james_veitch_spam": [
    {
      "start": 12.81,
      "end": 17.44,
      "text": "A few years ago, I got one of those spam emails."
    },
    {
      "start": 17.47,
      "end": 19.57,
      "text": "And it managed to get through my spam filter."
    },
    {
      "start": 19.6,
      "end": 24.33,
      "text": "I'm not quite sure how, but it turned up in my inbox, and it was from a guy called Solomon Odonkoh."
    },
    {
      "start": 24.35,
      "end": 25.57,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 26.16,
      "end": 27.31,
      "text": "I know."
    },
    {
      "start": 27.33,
      "end": 28.56,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 28.59,
      "end": 29.75,
      "text": "It went like this:"
    },
    {
      "start": 29.77,
      "end": 34.95,
      "text": "It said, \"Hello James Veitch, I have an interesting business proposal I want to share with you, Solomon.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 36.25,
      "end": 39.16,
      "text": "Now, my hand was kind of hovering on the delete button, right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 39.18,
      "end": 42.14,
      "text": "I was looking at my phone."
    },
    {
      "start": 39.18,
      "end": 46.61,
      "text": "I thought, I could just delete this, or I could do what I think we've all always wanted to do."
    },
    {
      "start": 46.63,
      "end": 49.62,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 50.54,
      "end": 52.75,
      "text": "And I said, \"Solomon, Your email intrigues me.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 52.77,
      "end": 55.47,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 55.5,
      "end": 60.48,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 60.5,
      "end": 62.92,
      "text": "And the game was afoot."
    },
    {
      "start": 65.21,
      "end": 68.17,
      "text": "He said, \"Dear James Veitch, We shall be shipping Gold to you.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 68.2,
      "end": 69.9,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 70.3,
      "end": 73.2,
      "text": "\"You will earn 10% of any gold you distributes.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 73.22,
      "end": 74.98,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 75.31,
      "end": 77.36,
      "text": "So I knew I was dealing with a professional."
    },
    {
      "start": 77.38,
      "end": 80.96,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 82.41,
      "end": 83.89,
      "text": "I said, \"How much is it worth?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 85.06,
      "end": 91.55,
      "text": "He said, \"We will start with smaller quantity,\" -- I was like, aww -- and then he said, \"of 25 kgs."
    },
    {
      "start": 91.58,
      "end": 93.58,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 94.97,
      "end": 97.43,
      "text": "The worth should be about $2.5 million.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 99.54,
      "end": 114.19,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, if we're going to do it, let's go big. (Applause) I can handle it."
    },
    {
      "start": 110.29,
      "end": 114.19,
      "text": "How much gold do you have?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 114.22,
      "end": 117.31,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 117.76,
      "end": 124.68,
      "text": "He said, \"It is not a matter of how much gold I have, what matters is your capability of handling."
    },
    {
      "start": 122.46,
      "end": 124.68,
      "text": "We can start with 50 kgs as trial shipment.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 124.71,
      "end": 130.5,
      "text": "I said, \"50 kgs? There's no point doing this at all unless you're shipping at least a metric ton.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 130.52,
      "end": 131.72,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 131.74,
      "end": 134.51,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 135.4,
      "end": 137.32,
      "text": "He said, \"What do you do for a living?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 137.35,
      "end": 139.2,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 140.28,
      "end": 142.62,
      "text": "I said, \"I'm a hedge fund executive bank manager.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 142.65,
      "end": 146.06,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 147.24,
      "end": 151.64,
      "text": "This isn't the first time I've shipped bullion, my friend, no no no."
    },
    {
      "start": 152.99,
      "end": 154.14,
      "text": "Then I started to panic."
    },
    {
      "start": 154.16,
      "end": 155.79,
      "text": "I was like, \"Where are you based?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 155.82,
      "end": 161.92,
      "text": "I don't know about you, but I think if we're going via the postal service, it ought to be signed for."
    },
    {
      "start": 160.75,
      "end": 161.92,
      "text": "That's a lot of gold.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 162.67,
      "end": 166.84,
      "text": "He said, \"It will not be easy to convince my company to do larger quantity shipment.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 166.86,
      "end": 174.06,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, I'm completely with you on this one."
    },
    {
      "start": 169.41,
      "end": 174.06,
      "text": "I'm putting together a visual for you to take into the board meeting."
    },
    {
      "start": 172.88,
      "end": 174.06,
      "text": "Hold tight.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 174.09,
      "end": 177.12,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 181.01,
      "end": 182.36,
      "text": "This is what I sent Solomon."
    },
    {
      "start": 183.21,
      "end": 185.34,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 185.36,
      "end": 191.27,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 191.83,
      "end": 196.92,
      "text": "I don't know if we have any statisticians in the house, but there's definitely something going on."
    },
    {
      "start": 196.94,
      "end": 198.57,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 199.17,
      "end": 202.45,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, attached to this email you'll find a helpful chart."
    },
    {
      "start": 203.28,
      "end": 205.57,
      "text": "I've had one of my assistants run the numbers."
    },
    {
      "start": 205.6,
      "end": 207.39,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 207.41,
      "end": 209.79,
      "text": "We're ready for shipping as much gold as possible.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 210.25,
      "end": 214.67,
      "text": "There's always a moment where they try to tug your heartstrings, and this was it for Solomon."
    },
    {
      "start": 214.69,
      "end": 220.02,
      "text": "He said, \"I will be so much happy if the deal goes well, because I'm going to get a very good commission as well.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 220.04,
      "end": 223.33,
      "text": "And I said, \"That's amazing, What are you going to spend your cut on?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 223.36,
      "end": 225.51,
      "text": "And he said, \"On RealEstate, what about you?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 226.13,
      "end": 229.18,
      "text": "I thought about it for a long time."
    },
    {
      "start": 230.93,
      "end": 234.78,
      "text": "And I said, \"One word; Hummus.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 234.81,
      "end": 237.99,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 240.26,
      "end": 241.41,
      "text": "\"It's going places."
    },
    {
      "start": 241.44,
      "end": 242.62,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 243.4,
      "end": 247.11,
      "text": "I was in Sainsbury's the other day and there were like 30 different varieties."
    },
    {
      "start": 247.14,
      "end": 250.04,
      "text": "Also you can cut up carrots, and you can dip them."
    },
    {
      "start": 250.06,
      "end": 251.92,
      "text": "Have you ever done that, Solomon?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 251.94,
      "end": 253.94,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 254.46,
      "end": 255.99,
      "text": "He said, \"I have to go bed now.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 256.01,
      "end": 258.89,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 258.92,
      "end": 261.78,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 261.8,
      "end": 263.18,
      "text": "\"Till morrow."
    },
    {
      "start": 264.41,
      "end": 265.56,
      "text": "Have sweet dream.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 265.58,
      "end": 266.97,
      "text": "I didn't know what to say!"
    },
    {
      "start": 267.64,
      "end": 270.53,
      "text": "I said, \"Bonsoir my golden nugget, bonsoir.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 270.55,
      "end": 272.85,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 273.32,
      "end": 280.3,
      "text": "Guys, you have to understand, this had been going for, like, weeks, albeit hitherto the greatest weeks of my life, but I had to knock it on the head."
    },
    {
      "start": 280.32,
      "end": 281.9,
      "text": "It was getting a bit out of hand."
    },
    {
      "start": 281.93,
      "end": 287.92,
      "text": "Friends were saying, \"James, do you want to come for a drink?\" I was like, \"I can't, I'm expecting an email about some gold.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 287.94,
      "end": 293.24,
      "text": "So I figured I had to knock it on the head, I had to take it to a ridiculous conclusion, so I concocted a plan."
    },
    {
      "start": 293.27,
      "end": 298.03,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, I'm concerned about security, when we email each other, we need to use a code.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 300.58,
      "end": 301.73,
      "text": "And he agreed."
    },
    {
      "start": 301.76,
      "end": 303.15,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 303.17,
      "end": 322.28,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, I spent all night coming up with this code we need to use in all further correspondence: Lawyer: Gummy Bear, Bank: Cream Egg, Legal: Fizzy Cola Bottle, Claim: Peanut M&Ms, Documents: Jelly Beans, Western Union: A Giant Gummy Lizard.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 322.31,
      "end": 324.14,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 324.6,
      "end": 326.65,
      "text": "I knew these were all words they use, right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 326.96,
      "end": 330.63,
      "text": "I said, \"Please call me Kitkat in all further correspondence.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 330.66,
      "end": 338.43,
      "text": "I didn't hear back. I thought, I've gone too far."
    },
    {
      "start": 338.45,
      "end": 340.79,
      "text": "I've gone too far. So I had to backpedal a little."
    },
    {
      "start": 340.82,
      "end": 345.46,
      "text": "I said, \"Solomon, Is the deal still on? KitKat.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 345.49,
      "end": 346.74,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 346.76,
      "end": 348.63,
      "text": "Because you have to be consistent."
    },
    {
      "start": 351.21,
      "end": 356.28,
      "text": "Then I did get an email back from him. He said, \"The Business is on and I am trying to blah blah blah ...\""
    },
    {
      "start": 356.3,
      "end": 358.28,
      "text": "I said, \"Dude, you have to use the code!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 361.48,
      "end": 364.07,
      "text": "What followed is the greatest email I've ever received."
    },
    {
      "start": 364.09,
      "end": 366.27,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 367.68,
      "end": 370.23,
      "text": "I'm not joking, this is what turned up in my inbox."
    },
    {
      "start": 370.94,
      "end": 372.09,
      "text": "This was a good day."
    },
    {
      "start": 373.22,
      "end": 405.28,
      "text": "\"The business is on. I am trying to raise the balance for the Gummy Bear -- (Laughter) so he can submit all the needed Fizzy Cola Bottle Jelly Beans to the Creme Egg, for the Peanut M&Ms process to start. (Laughter) Send 1,500 pounds via a Giant Gummy Lizard.\" (Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 409.39,
      "end": 413.17,
      "text": "And that was so much fun, right, that it got me thinking:"
    },
    {
      "start": 413.19,
      "end": 418.22,
      "text": "Like, what would happen if I just spent as much time as could replying to as many scam emails as I could?"
    },
    {
      "start": 420.14,
      "end": 426.55,
      "text": "And that's what I've been doing for three years on your behalf."
    },
    {
      "start": 426.57,
      "end": 428.97,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 428.99,
      "end": 431.31,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 432.08,
      "end": 434.95,
      "text": "Crazy stuff happens when you start replying to scam emails."
    },
    {
      "start": 434.97,
      "end": 437.73,
      "text": "It's really difficult, and I highly recommend we do it."
    },
    {
      "start": 437.75,
      "end": 439.59,
      "text": "I don't think what I'm doing is mean."
    },
    {
      "start": 439.62,
      "end": 442.37,
      "text": "There are a lot of people who do mean things to scammers."
    },
    {
      "start": 442.39,
      "end": 445.98,
      "text": "All I'm doing is wasting their time."
    },
    {
      "start": 446.89,
      "end": 453.07,
      "text": "And I think any time they're spending with me is time they're not spending scamming vulnerable adults out of their savings, right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 453.58,
      "end": 459.18,
      "text": "And if you're going to do this -- and I highly recommend you do -- get yourself a pseudonymous email address."
    },
    {
      "start": 459.21,
      "end": 460.81,
      "text": "Don't use your own email address."
    },
    {
      "start": 460.83,
      "end": 463.68,
      "text": "That's what I was doing at the start and it was a nightmare."
    },
    {
      "start": 463.71,
      "end": 473.2,
      "text": "I'd wake up in the morning and have a thousand emails about penis enlargements, only one of which was a legitimate response -- (Laughter) to a medical question I had."
    },
    {
      "start": 473.23,
      "end": 480.47,
      "text": "But I'll tell you what, though, guys, I'll tell you what: any day is a good day, any day is a good day if you receive an email that begins like this:"
    },
    {
      "start": 481.56,
      "end": 484.37,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 484.4,
      "end": 490.38,
      "text": "\"I AM WINNIE MANDELA, THE SECOND WIFE OF NELSON MANDELA THE FORMER SOUTH AFRICAN PRESIDENT.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 490.41,
      "end": 492.31,
      "text": "I was like, oh! -- that Winnie Mandela."
    },
    {
      "start": 492.34,
      "end": 493.42,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 493.44,
      "end": 494.59,
      "text": "I know so many."
    },
    {
      "start": 495.55,
      "end": 501.4,
      "text": "\"I NEED TO TRANSFER 45 MILLION DOLLARS OUT OF THE COUNTRY BECAUSE OF MY HUSBAND NELSON MANDELA'S HEALTH CONDITION.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 501.42,
      "end": 502.58,
      "text": "Let that sink in."
    },
    {
      "start": 503.86,
      "end": 506.5,
      "text": "She sent me this, which is hysterical."
    },
    {
      "start": 506.53,
      "end": 509.48,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 509.5,
      "end": 510.66,
      "text": "And this."
    },
    {
      "start": 510.68,
      "end": 513.88,
      "text": "And this looks fairly legitimate, this is a letter of authorization."
    },
    {
      "start": 513.9,
      "end": 517.29,
      "text": "But to be honest, if there's nothing written on it, it's just a shape!"
    },
    {
      "start": 517.32,
      "end": 519.17,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 519.65,
      "end": 526.62,
      "text": "I said, \"Winnie, I'm really sorry to hear of this. Given that Nelson died three months ago, I'd describe his health condition as fairly serious.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 526.64,
      "end": 529.45,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 529.48,
      "end": 533.0,
      "text": "That's the worst health condition you can have, not being alive."
    },
    {
      "start": 535.37,
      "end": 539.33,
      "text": "She said, \"KINDLY COMPLY WITH MY BANKERS INSTRUCTIONS. ONE LOVE.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 539.35,
      "end": 541.3,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 542.33,
      "end": 544.15,
      "text": "I said, \"Of course. NO WOMAN, NO CRY.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 544.17,
      "end": 547.67,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 547.7,
      "end": 551.2,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 552.7,
      "end": 555.85,
      "text": "She said, \"MY BANKER WILL NEED TRANSFER OF 3000 DOLLARS. ONE LOVE.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 555.88,
      "end": 558.01,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 560.01,
      "end": 565.69,
      "text": "I said, \"no problemo. I SHOT THE SHERIFF.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 569.81,
      "end": 571.69,
      "text": "[ (BUT I DID NOT SHOOT THE DEPUTY) ]"
    },
    {
      "start": 571.72,
      "end": 572.74,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 572.77,
      "end": 573.92,
      "text": "Thank you."
    },
    {
      "start": 573.94,
      "end": 587.2,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    }
  ],
  "james_veitch_unsubscribe": [
    {
      "start": 12.98,
      "end": 14.79,
      "text": "It's funny the things you forget."
    },
    {
      "start": 15.81,
      "end": 34.12,
      "text": "I went to see my mother the other day, and she told me this story that I'd completely forgotten about how, when we were driving together, she would pull the car over, and by the time she had gotten out of the car, and gone around the car to let me out of the car, I would have already gotten out of the car and pretended to have died."
    },
    {
      "start": 34.14,
      "end": 37.09,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 37.11,
      "end": 41.46,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 47.39,
      "end": 48.91,
      "text": "Because that's how you die."
    },
    {
      "start": 48.93,
      "end": 50.86,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 50.88,
      "end": 57.41,
      "text": "And I remember, that was a game I used to play with myself to entertain myself whenever I was bored or frustrated."
    },
    {
      "start": 58.17,
      "end": 59.57,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 60.17,
      "end": 61.51,
      "text": "Settle down."
    },
    {
      "start": 61.54,
      "end": 63.53,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 63.93,
      "end": 66.82,
      "text": "People say we live in an age of information overload. Right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 66.84,
      "end": 70.59,
      "text": "I don't know about that, but I just know that I get too many marketing emails."
    },
    {
      "start": 70.62,
      "end": 79.37,
      "text": "I got a marketing email from a supermarket firm, which will remain nameless for predominantly legal reasons, but which I'm going to call \"SafeMart.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 79.4,
      "end": 81.46,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 81.48,
      "end": 88.94,
      "text": "I got an email from them, and it went like this, it said: \"Just three weeks until SafeMart at King's Cross opens!!!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 88.97,
      "end": 100.47,
      "text": "And I resented this, because not only do I not remember signing up to that, but I resent the fact that they appear to think that I should be excited about a shop opening."
    },
    {
      "start": 101.26,
      "end": 105.83,
      "text": "So what I did was I scrolled down to the bottom of the email, and I pressed, \"Unsubscribe.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 106.24,
      "end": 108.07,
      "text": "And I thought that'd be the end of it."
    },
    {
      "start": 108.09,
      "end": 114.58,
      "text": "But a week later, I got another one that said, \"Just two weeks until SafeMart at King's Cross opens!!!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 114.6,
      "end": 117.23,
      "text": "And I thought, obviously, I haven't clicked hard enough."
    },
    {
      "start": 117.25,
      "end": 119.14,
      "text": "So I tried it again. Right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 120.25,
      "end": 126.82,
      "text": "Lo and behold, a week passes, you guessed it, \"Just one week until SafeMart at King's Cross opens!!!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 126.84,
      "end": 128.68,
      "text": "And here's the problem:"
    },
    {
      "start": 128.71,
      "end": 134.19,
      "text": "The internet gave us access to everything; but it also gave everything access to us."
    },
    {
      "start": 135.18,
      "end": 145.04,
      "text": "It's hard enough to discriminate between the things that genuinely matter in this world and the minutiae of life, without having emails about supermarket chains and Candy Crush Saga."
    },
    {
      "start": 146.7,
      "end": 152.89,
      "text": "And I was really annoyed with them, and I thought, OK, I was about to write a strongly worded email, which I can do quite well."
    },
    {
      "start": 152.91,
      "end": 154.7,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 155.14,
      "end": 158.95,
      "text": "And I thought, no -- I'm going to find the game."
    },
    {
      "start": 158.97,
      "end": 162.9,
      "text": "So I replied to it, and I said, \"I literally cannot wait!!!!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 163.53,
      "end": 165.45,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 167.17,
      "end": 168.69,
      "text": "\"What do you need from me?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 171.2,
      "end": 173.21,
      "text": "They got back to me; a guy called Dan said,"
    },
    {
      "start": 173.24,
      "end": 176.15,
      "text": "\"Hi James. I've asked a colleague to help me with your query.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 176.17,
      "end": 177.19,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 177.22,
      "end": 178.85,
      "text": "Like it needs help."
    },
    {
      "start": 180.36,
      "end": 184.43,
      "text": "And I said, \"What's the plan, Dan? I'm thinking fireworks, bouncy castle...\""
    },
    {
      "start": 184.46,
      "end": 186.65,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 188.28,
      "end": 189.76,
      "text": "\"I'm not sure what you mean.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 189.78,
      "end": 190.99,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 191.51,
      "end": 194.31,
      "text": "I said, \"I'm just tremendously excited about the opening!\""
    },
    {
      "start": 194.33,
      "end": 195.77,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 197.36,
      "end": 200.89,
      "text": "\"Do you want to book the bouncy castle or shall I?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 203.69,
      "end": 205.84,
      "text": "He said, \"I think you have misunderstood.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 205.86,
      "end": 207.37,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 207.4,
      "end": 210.39,
      "text": "\"A new store is opening, but there is no celebration planned.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 211.6,
      "end": 217.74,
      "text": "I said, \"But what was all the 'Three weeks until,' 'Two weeks until' emails? I was getting excited.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 217.77,
      "end": 219.25,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 220.3,
      "end": 221.85,
      "text": "\"I'm sorry you're disappointed.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 221.87,
      "end": 223.87,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 225.69,
      "end": 234.37,
      "text": "I said, \"Not to worry. Let's do something anyway! Besides, the deposit on the bouncy castle was non-refundable.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 234.39,
      "end": 236.49,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 238.81,
      "end": 241.63,
      "text": "\"If we don't use it, we're out a few hundred quid, Dan.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 241.66,
      "end": 243.35,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 244.72,
      "end": 248.25,
      "text": "He said, \"Mr. Veitch, I'm not responsible for anything you have ordered.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 249.45,
      "end": 256.81,
      "text": "I said, \"Let's not get into who did what. Bottom line: you and I are in this together.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 256.83,
      "end": 259.07,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 259.1,
      "end": 260.73,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 263.45,
      "end": 266.83,
      "text": "\"Question: Will you be there to make sure people take their shoes off?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 266.85,
      "end": 268.98,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 271.43,
      "end": 277.23,
      "text": "I'll be honest, then my relationship with Dan deteriorated somewhat, because the next email I got was this:"
    },
    {
      "start": 277.25,
      "end": 279.63,
      "text": "\"Thanks for your email - your Case Number is ...\""
    },
    {
      "start": 279.65,
      "end": 281.65,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 281.98,
      "end": 283.13,
      "text": "That's outrageous."
    },
    {
      "start": 283.16,
      "end": 284.31,
      "text": "I said, \"Dan?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 284.33,
      "end": 286.17,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 286.19,
      "end": 289.11,
      "text": "And I got -- and I was just like, this is ... -- and I, I ...."
    },
    {
      "start": 289.14,
      "end": 290.94,
      "text": "And I said, \"Danny?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 291.41,
      "end": 296.03,
      "text": "And I thought, this is terrible. All I'm doing is collecting case numbers."
    },
    {
      "start": 296.05,
      "end": 297.6,
      "text": "I said, \"D-Dog?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 297.62,
      "end": 299.45,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 300.03,
      "end": 301.33,
      "text": "\"The store is now open.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 301.35,
      "end": 303.35,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 305.1,
      "end": 309.61,
      "text": "I said, \"But Dan, they must have wondered why there was no bouncy castle.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 310.59,
      "end": 312.9,
      "text": "And then we were back to this."
    },
    {
      "start": 315.12,
      "end": 327.36,
      "text": "And that might have been the end of the story, but I remembered that anything -- everything -- even something as mundane as getting out of a car, can be fun if you find the right game."
    },
    {
      "start": 329.47,
      "end": 333.25,
      "text": "So, this is what I replied:"
    },
    {
      "start": 334.06,
      "end": 336.67,
      "text": "[Thanks for your email - your Case Number is #0000001.]"
    },
    {
      "start": 336.69,
      "end": 338.98,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 339.0,
      "end": 342.33,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 344.84,
      "end": 346.24,
      "text": "And we just, uh ..."
    },
    {
      "start": 346.26,
      "end": 348.26,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 348.29,
      "end": 350.44,
      "text": "It was like we were dancing."
    },
    {
      "start": 350.46,
      "end": 352.7,
      "text": "It was just a beautiful relationship."
    },
    {
      "start": 352.73,
      "end": 355.13,
      "text": "We just kept going."
    },
    {
      "start": 355.15,
      "end": 356.59,
      "text": "It was lovely."
    },
    {
      "start": 356.62,
      "end": 363.98,
      "text": "But to be honest, guys, it was quite labor-intensive, and I had other stuff to do, believe it or not."
    },
    {
      "start": 365.26,
      "end": 370.99,
      "text": "So what I did is I have a little email auto-replier program."
    },
    {
      "start": 372.36,
      "end": 376.67,
      "text": "And I set it up so every time it receives an email from SafeMart, it just pings one back."
    },
    {
      "start": 376.69,
      "end": 380.43,
      "text": "So I set it up, and it says, \"Thanks for your email - your Case Number is ...\""
    },
    {
      "start": 380.45,
      "end": 384.3,
      "text": "Then it has a little formula that I wrote to up the case number every time."
    },
    {
      "start": 384.33,
      "end": 387.81,
      "text": "And I put it on the server and set it running."
    },
    {
      "start": 389.68,
      "end": 393.45,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 394.89,
      "end": 397.5,
      "text": "I'll be honest, guys -- then I forgot about it."
    },
    {
      "start": 397.52,
      "end": 399.52,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 400.45,
      "end": 408.9,
      "text": "I checked back on it the other day, and it appears there have been a number of emails going back and forth."
    },
    {
      "start": 409.66,
      "end": 412.64,
      "text": "We're on 21,439."
    },
    {
      "start": 412.67,
      "end": 416.85,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    },
    {
      "start": 419.25,
      "end": 427.74,
      "text": "It gives me an immense sense of satisfaction to know that these computer programs are just going to be pinging one another for eternity."
    },
    {
      "start": 428.93,
      "end": 431.28,
      "text": "And as legacies go, I don't think that's bad."
    },
    {
      "start": 433.57,
      "end": 443.89,
      "text": "So guys, just remember: if ever you feel weighed down by the bureaucracy and often mundanity of modern life, don't fight the frustration."
    },
    {
      "start": 446.26,
      "end": 449.9,
      "text": "Let it be the catalyst for whimsy."
    },
    {
      "start": 451.74,
      "end": 454.26,
      "text": "(Laughter)"
    },
    {
      "start": 454.28,
      "end": 455.44,
      "text": "Thank you."
    },
    {
      "start": 455.46,
      "end": 459.29,
      "text": "(Applause)"
    }
  ],
  "jeff_allen_teenagers": [
    {
      "start": 0.06,
      "end": 5.69,
      "text": "And that's what she turns her on. What'd you just say to me? I didn't say nothing."
    },
    {
      "start": 6.44,
      "end": 33.72,
      "text": "We've been married 32 years and I got my father gave me one really wise piece of advice before I got married, and it's held true for 32 years. On my wedding day, my father said to me, \"Before you argue with your new wife, and you're gonna argue with her, before you do, take some time, step back, ask yourself two questions: Do you want to be right or do you want to be happy?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 30.21,
      "end": 55.53,
      "text": "Right, and then he broke down and sobbed right in front of me. I had no idea what that man was talking about. 32 years later, I could tell you this: I'm a happy, happy, happy man. I have been right in 12 years now."
    },
    {
      "start": 48.03,
      "end": 63.72,
      "text": "Some say he's gonna have to ask her, \"Am I happy?\" Oh, you better believe you're happy. I was just checking with your buttercup."
    },
    {
      "start": 61.26,
      "end": 66.98,
      "text": "Call my friends up, I can't go golfing, but I'm a happy, happy, happy man."
    },
    {
      "start": 67.63,
      "end": 128.96,
      "text": "Don't get me wrong, we argue. You've got to argue in your marriage. You don't argue in your marriage, it'll build up in your brain over time and fries your brain, yeah, and then you wind up like those babbling, mumbling couples you've seen in Arizona, Florida. These fifty-plus years of marriage, they're kind of walking down the street, the wife is fine, it's the poor husband eight feet behind her that scares me to death. This poor man's all hunched over, he's vibrating, mumble, \"Is always telling me what to do. Start telling you what to do? I'm a man, you can't tell I'm a man? I'm a me.\" This poor guy started to try to win back all the arguments he's been throwing away for 50 years. You know, he was 63 when he got married, now he's four foot one. Look at the poor man, weighed down by half a century of apathy. Leave a toilet seat up if I want to leave a toilet. You tell me what to do? I hope you sit in the water every night. I don't. And that's what she turns her on. What'd you just say to me? I didn't say nothing."
    },
    {
      "start": 125.81,
      "end": 128.96,
      "text": "[Music]"
    },
    {
      "start": 129.78,
      "end": 155.97,
      "text": "Scary if to learn how to communicate. That's the word: communication. You have to learn how your spouse communicates. That takes time. Many women communicate different. It took me two years of marriage to figure out my wife will never tell me to do anything around our home. If Tammy wants me to do something, she'll ask me a question. It's from the question."
    },
    {
      "start": 154.14,
      "end": 165.93,
      "text": "That I got to stand there and figure out what if she wants me to do, simple example, say leave a pair of my underwear in the middle of the bedroom floor."
    },
    {
      "start": 162.96,
      "end": 175.85,
      "text": "Which frosts my wife — that's her word when she's angry — that just frosts me, Jeffrey."
    },
    {
      "start": 174.17,
      "end": 183.02,
      "text": "If I'm not frosted, I'm driving up a wall; that's another one."
    },
    {
      "start": 181.76,
      "end": 186.65,
      "text": "Kids had come in: \"Where's mom?\" \"She's up the wall.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 184.1,
      "end": 196.91,
      "text": "Of frostbite, that's all I know."
    },
    {
      "start": 194.09,
      "end": 203.45,
      "text": "You won't believe what put her there, man, was that pair of underwear in the middle of the bedroom."
    },
    {
      "start": 199.97,
      "end": 208.28,
      "text": "You're looking at the most powerful piece of cotton."
    },
    {
      "start": 206.3,
      "end": 218.42,
      "text": "I'm playing it over, so I leave my underwear in the middle of the room — would she come to me and say to me, \"Pick those up\"? That's three words. Hey, pick those up — three words."
    },
    {
      "start": 213.83,
      "end": 222.73,
      "text": "What'd she say? No, because that would be simple, direct, and right to the point."
    },
    {
      "start": 224.51,
      "end": 236.75,
      "text": "And at that moment, we would be communicating at the highest human level, the way God the Creator intended it — through language."
    },
    {
      "start": 236.15,
      "end": 244.31,
      "text": "She looks at me, looks at my underwear, and then asks, \"Are those yours?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 238.88,
      "end": 248.27,
      "text": "I sure hope they aren't, otherwise I got a few questions of my own."
    },
    {
      "start": 245.75,
      "end": 253.63,
      "text": "\"What do you want?\" That's the only question a man has in his life: what do you want?"
    },
    {
      "start": 250.46,
      "end": 256.82,
      "text": "But talking in code? Tell me what you want — my favorite question."
    },
    {
      "start": 255.74,
      "end": 269.42,
      "text": "We weren't married two months; I'm leaving the house, I got golf clubs on my shoulder, got golf shoes in my hand, and everybody knows what she asked me: \"Where are you going?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 268.01,
      "end": 276.53,
      "text": "I was only married a couple months, I didn't know any better; I looked at her and said, \"I'm going bowling,\" Colombo — if you're taking notes, that would be the wrong answer."
    },
    {
      "start": 275.6,
      "end": 283.67,
      "text": "An hour later, I was still in my living room: \"Come on, tell me, what is this about? Please, let me know; I can make the back nine, just let me.\" It's about knowing the right answer."
    },
    {
      "start": 282.08,
      "end": 286.91,
      "text": "That's why — why your beautiful, intelligent wife would ask such a banal question."
    },
    {
      "start": 285.05,
      "end": 304.06,
      "text": "I know better today; I'm leaving the house with golf clubs on my shoulder today, and Tammy says, \"Where are you going?\" \"We'll put these in the car, baby; I'm gonna come back and mow our lawn.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 301.54,
      "end": 308.47,
      "text": "Just practice! And leaving for golf about eight or nine years ago, my wife and I picked up my oldest boy at Fort Campbell."
    },
    {
      "start": 307.0,
      "end": 318.31,
      "text": "He did six years, he served six years with the 101st Airborne, so we picked him up and we're driving back — we live in Nashville; it's..."
    },
    {
      "start": 316.87,
      "end": 323.5,
      "text": "about an hour-and-a-half drive so we're driving back and I asked my son now that you're out of the military you've had"
    },
    {
      "start": 321.55,
      "end": 335.85,
      "text": "six years to think about it what are your plans and he said well it's kind of hoping I can move back home while re-acclimate to civilian life I said so you haven't thought about it have you no"
    },
    {
      "start": 333.67,
      "end": 346.69,
      "text": "I said all right well you've returned it take all the time you need 19 months into the acclimating process my wife"
    },
    {
      "start": 344.86,
      "end": 356.71,
      "text": "grabs me in the hall one afternoon how long does it take to acclimate I said I don't know I never had to go downstairs and talk to that boy so I'm went"
    },
    {
      "start": 354.22,
      "end": 366.25,
      "text": "downstairs I politely asked my 26 year old son pause your video game I need your full attention on this one Maya and I are a little curious did the army"
    },
    {
      "start": 364.54,
      "end": 371.77,
      "text": "teach you a skill something and used to get employment and move out I want you to understand something young man your"
    },
    {
      "start": 370.12,
      "end": 380.05,
      "text": "mother and I want grandchildren we've earned grandchildren and we worship a God of miracles we believe on a three billion women on this planet God has"
    },
    {
      "start": 378.58,
      "end": 392.16,
      "text": "chosen one of those women for you to breed with we just don't think she's gonna fall through our vent that'll land in your"
    },
    {
      "start": 387.27,
      "end": 399.45,
      "text": "lap down here in the basement so she learned anything he thought about it he said you know I could kill you six ways"
    },
    {
      "start": 396.03,
      "end": 411.66,
      "text": "with a popsicle stick you enjoyed it video game my man and what mom and they know when you're done doing that acclimating thing so I run upstairs and Tammy says to me how what'd he say"
    },
    {
      "start": 409.71,
      "end": 415.43,
      "text": "he said get rid of the fudgesicles cuz he's going on a diet that's what he said"
    },
    {
      "start": 418.92,
      "end": 427.56,
      "text": "I don't know I don't know if the army fed him for six years he was literally eating us out of house and my wife and I"
    },
    {
      "start": 425.31,
      "end": 436.86,
      "text": "started hiding food in our bedroom to keep it from our son about why that's pathetic it really is we had a stash of brownies and muffins"
    },
    {
      "start": 433.44,
      "end": 443.4,
      "text": "in the nightstand next to us so late at night we get all excited run down the hall hold hands lock the bedroom door"
    },
    {
      "start": 441.41,
      "end": 454.83,
      "text": "I'm sure he thought we were doing something else but weird we were just under the covers eating brownies and laughing at him really it 50 your life"
    },
    {
      "start": 452.13,
      "end": 466.98,
      "text": "gets pathetic it really does the brownies are here we've run down the hall lock the door hurry he's gonna smell these and what when I know it two"
    },
    {
      "start": 462.6,
      "end": 471.84,
      "text": "hours and that is and the truth be known that's all we do in our bed anymore"
    },
    {
      "start": 468.6,
      "end": 487.17,
      "text": "Anyway, Izzie, we bought a Select Comfort but I don't know if you're familiar with it but each side of the bed has its own number; the higher the number, the harder the mattress; my side of the bed, 100, marble slab, that's it."
    },
    {
      "start": 482.87,
      "end": 511.14,
      "text": "Tami's numbers – the first night she laid on her side of the bed, she literally disappeared from my view; the mattress wrapped around her like a flour tortilla; the poor thing was sleeping on a fajita on her side of the bed, so I roll over to kiss her goodnight and I fall into a ditch; I can't get out; on my way in there, she's gonna get off of me; what are you doing over here? Get off of me."
    },
    {
      "start": 508.52,
      "end": 517.39,
      "text": "By the way, if you're a newlywed, that's the sound of 32 years of love right there."
    },
    {
      "start": 514.14,
      "end": 517.39,
      "text": "[Music]"
    },
    {
      "start": 518.63,
      "end": 525.85,
      "text": "Yes, you're on my hair; you haven't shaved; your breath smells; stop touching me."
    },
    {
      "start": 527.65,
      "end": 538.45,
      "text": "Don't get me wrong, we still try to get romantic, we just save it for special holidays now; turns out Tami's favorite is leap year."
    },
    {
      "start": 542.31,
      "end": 548.14,
      "text": "I didn't even know that was a holiday; I really do."
    },
    {
      "start": 546.16,
      "end": 561.16,
      "text": "I'll dim the lights and put on some Shaddai, and Tami says, \"Wow, has it been four years already? Alright, I'll get the good flannel and the hunting socks out tonight.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 555.19,
      "end": 571.24,
      "text": "Now we're married, we try, we just know how much time and energy it actually takes, and we prefer to watch Law & Order; that's just the way it is."
    },
    {
      "start": 568.57,
      "end": 580.11,
      "text": "My wife falls asleep at 9:15 every night; you could set a watch by her; by 9:10 you look at her on the couch in our living room, it's like a gas leak goes off."
    },
    {
      "start": 585.13,
      "end": 599.79,
      "text": "Sweetheart, maybe you should go to bed. I'm fine, I'm fine; what are you, a five-year-old? Quit fighting it, just go to bed."
    },
    {
      "start": 594.93,
      "end": 611.72,
      "text": "Do you be quiet; this is my favorite show; for a woman's scene, the beginning of every Law & Order for five seasons and now they come out in rerun, and she's yelling at me."
    },
    {
      "start": 609.77,
      "end": 613.93,
      "text": "I've seen this one; we'll give it 20 minutes."
    },
    {
      "start": 619.39,
      "end": 626.56,
      "text": "I love that woman."
    },
    {
      "start": 623.97,
      "end": 644.11,
      "text": "People ask – they asked me, \"Is your wife aware the way you talk about her?\" No, so don't be Facebooking her; this is her idea, funny. God blessed me with an amazing woman; she's got a dark twisted sense of humor, I mean it; she's done this."
    },
    {
      "start": 642.55,
      "end": 649.02,
      "text": "Is her idea funny? I'm in my chair at home. Every man, you have a chair, don't you? Serve."
    },
    {
      "start": 645.79,
      "end": 663.36,
      "text": "Don't write me. We have a check, your man, that's your throne. If you ever went missing, they give a cushion in that chair to a bloodhound. Wouldn't they find that smell? And then they provide the dog and send him on his way."
    },
    {
      "start": 664.26,
      "end": 669.95,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 666.82,
      "end": 680.53,
      "text": "So I'm in my chair one afternoon minding my own business. Now my wife comes up behind me, she starts rubbing me like that. She's got her arms around me like that, she's nibbling at my ear, and of course I'm thinking: Is it February 29th?"
    },
    {
      "start": 683.01,
      "end": 760.14,
      "text": "It was a nice moment between a man and wife; we were married 20-plus years at that point, and it's a nice moment in like a few minutes, just really nice and tender. And then at one point she kisses the top of my head and she hugs me, she said, \"I love you, Jeffrey.\" Said, \"I love you too, baby,\" and she walks away, and I'm basking in the warmth of this. I mean, 10-15 times I would yell, she was in the kitchen, \"Baby, that was nice! Thank you!\" And at one point my son comes walking through: \"Hey Dad, who drew that big smiley face on your balls?\" Fire yeah! What I thought was fingernails was a sharpie. She was bent to scratch it out. Look at my dog! So don't you feel sorry for her. You got to know your love language. I read a book; some guy wrote a book, Dr. Gary Chapman wrote a book called \"Five Love Languages.\" According to Dr. Gary Chapman, there are five languages of love between a man and wife. Tammy and I read that book twice in one week because we didn't see our love language in there. Yeah, apparently bitterness, sarcasm not part of Dr. Gary Chapman's love life."
    },
    {
      "start": 753.11,
      "end": 784.1,
      "text": "So I noticed my parents had their own love language; 57 years they were married before my mother passed away. That last Christmas we knew would be mom's last Christmas, so it was a special one; we knew this would be it. So anyway, we were all sitting around watching television, two movies: \"Wonderful Life,\" those Christmas movies. My mom's doing her crochet, my dad's in his chair, and he starts serenading my mother in their love language, 57 years of marriage."
    },
    {
      "start": 785.91,
      "end": 789.1,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 791.23,
      "end": 801.47,
      "text": "Oh! My mother didn't miss a beat, she didn't say anything. She puts her crochet down, she goes into the kitchen, comes back 20..."
    },
    {
      "start": 799.88,
      "end": 807.7,
      "text": "Minutes later with two hot dogs and a soda pop for her puzzle, how cool is that?"
    },
    {
      "start": 807.91,
      "end": 849.05,
      "text": "So that night I'm laying in the fajita with Tammy and I'm talking, I said, \"Did you see that today?\" She goes, \"What do I, parents did you see that whole thing?\" She goes, \"I did my good. Do you think we'll ever get there, you and me on that level?\" Tammy says, \"Shoot me if we do. You start hocking hair balls at me like that, it's over.\" That was one of the most vile, disgusting things I've ever witnessed in my life. I almost chucked my dinner all over the kid's head. Wow, I thought it was beautiful. I married a tough one."
    },
    {
      "start": 846.56,
      "end": 981.5,
      "text": "My Tammy's a tough, tough woman. First thing she did when we got married was take my spine away from me. She did. She keeps it in her purse. It's handy in case I have to do something. Mainly three o'clock in the morning I heard a noise. \"Here's your spine, go down and see.\" Little I hate that wake-up call. See here's the noise. I got to go down to see what it is. Well, if you were sleeping, you wouldn't have heard it. So go back to sleep. You won't hear the next one. Trust me, he's trying to be as quiet as he can. He'll probably apologize for waking us out. She's going on her feet, track mud on her new hardwood floor. She'd rip his thyroid out. She's got a temper. I mean a temper. I'm not talking hurt ability and sarcasm because that's what attracted me to her. Here's an example. We were eating breakfast one morning, one morning just sit at the breakfast, minding my own business, whistling. That's the kind of good mood I was in, whistling. Could explain why I didn't hear her ticking on the other side of the table. I just wanted to butter waffle, you know? What I'm talking about? You just want a butter walk, no family business, no talk, no nothing, butter a waffle. So I said, \"Sweetheart, could you pass me the butter knife?\" Of course, now I'm thinking something must be bothering Buttercup, so I asked, \"Something on your mind?\" She says, \"I'm fat.\" There's not a man in this room knows you can't respond to that. There's a twitch of the eye or get you killed at this point. If you're a newlywed, trust me. Your wife ever says she's fat, you become a mannequin. You don't move a muscle. You certainly don't say anything. Let's let her finish her thought."
    },
    {
      "start": 977.12,
      "end": 985.73,
      "text": "A knife on the wall, butter the wall then she says we're joining a health club."
    },
    {
      "start": 983.3,
      "end": 987.08,
      "text": "Did you hear what I said?"
    },
    {
      "start": 985.73,
      "end": 1001.18,
      "text": "I think I heard you say you're fat and we're joining the health club."
    },
    {
      "start": 988.66,
      "end": 1002.47,
      "text": "That was the wrong answer of course."
    },
    {
      "start": 1001.18,
      "end": 1026.07,
      "text": "We joined a health club and if there are two people on this planet that should have never purchased a health club membership, it is Tammy and I. Trust me, I haven't moved with any purpose since I won the race at conception. Trust me, I don't want to brag but I outran about four billion that morning and I'm still exhausted."
    },
    {
      "start": 1023.16,
      "end": 1036.95,
      "text": "We're lazy people. We're not ashamed of it."
    },
    {
      "start": 1035.16,
      "end": 1070.4,
      "text": "We've actually woken our children up to get the remote control for the television. We've had an exercise bike in our bedroom for ten years. It's got about a mile and a half on it and most of that was put on by the grandkids sitting on the floor just spending the pedals. She once called me from the driveway to get her purse. Hello, get my purse. Where you at? I'm in the driveway. I'm going to the health club and I need my ID. But ways you're you. Hey, go get your mother's purse and bring it to her."
    },
    {
      "start": 1068.25,
      "end": 1075.15,
      "text": "[Music]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1068.25,
      "end": 1075.15,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1077.43,
      "end": 1110.31,
      "text": "We've tried, you know. I've tried things when I was in my 20s. I tried skiing when I was actually corn. I got knocked unconscious by the chairlift. I mean, that's funny now. If that wasn't humiliating enough, the insurance company that I had it that wouldn't cover my head injury. I'm gonna make enough guy called me up at home. Said you got hit in the head with a chairlift. They said yes, sir. He goes, \"Wow, that makes you a [__]\" and we consider then a pre-existing condition. They had me was a loophole."
    },
    {
      "start": 1110.32,
      "end": 1135.43,
      "text": "I wanted to get in shape. I wanted to give these guys grandchildren. Now they're running me ragged. By the way, if you're a young parent, everything I was told about grandchildren is true. It is. They are your reward for what your teenagers live. That's fight the urge. Let those ingrates live and you'll bring you a bundle of joy."
    },
    {
      "start": 1130.96,
      "end": 1147.04,
      "text": "They will which is your time but you've never been sicker in your life. You spend time around little children. You know that they go to preschool. Why the CDC hasn't shut down every preschool in America? I'm not their little germ manga."
    },
    {
      "start": 1145.33,
      "end": 1161.31,
      "text": "I repeat, you think if you went to an American preschool, shut off all the lights, turn on a blacklight, it would look like the surface of the Sun in that place, and they're 5 years old, they don't know any better, they give stuff to each other, give this one your Papa."
    },
    {
      "start": 1163.39,
      "end": 1194.53,
      "text": "And still wait till I'm sleeping on the couch that she just stares at me while I'm laying there, and you know because of gravity, everything in her little mouth is flashing on my face, it's like sleeping under a toxic rain pipe, vanilla wafers and Kool-Aid did this paste falling on your face as soon as I open an eyeball, she goes, \"Oh, is that Ebola?\" I haven't had that one yet."
    },
    {
      "start": 1193.03,
      "end": 1250.89,
      "text": "And the worst is when they take your drink out of your water, they handed a glass you got a six-course meal flour on it, no pop, I'll get a new water, water shouldn't have 40 grams of fiber at this, and they live nearby which is really really cool, we see him a lot, and my son brought her over one day, he thought it'd be funny to teach my five-year-old granddaughter to give me a wet willy, that's funny now, so I got her on my lap and I'm bouncing around, she's licking her finger, no, and it's kind of amusing, you know, 30 seconds into it she goes, \"Pop, I got pinkeye,\" what squirt and parelle in my eye, it was cool, she just turned five, we had the birthday at our house and it was really kind of cool."
    },
    {
      "start": 1249.15,
      "end": 1314.31,
      "text": "And my son dropped her off and they had to go get her a gift, the bicycle or whatever it was, they didn't want it there so they drop her off about an hour before the party starts, and she sees all the balloons and everything, she knows it's her birthday, she's five, so she runs down, I'm on the couch, I'm reading a book, she's running down the hall, I heard a girl and she disappeared, I didn't see her, and then I heard her at plowin' oil wall, and I only tell you this because people after the show will ask me what sort of stuff makes you laugh, that would be one of them right there, right sorry, up side reading the books he disappears, I heard, that's funny, I'm sorry, that's funny, I tried to comfort her but I couldn't stop laughing at Evelyn, you were moving pop up, and when they cry at that age they get that yo-yo, it gets about here and you get transfixed on the yo-yo, and then the bubble shoots off, did that come?"
    },
    {
      "start": 1312.96,
      "end": 1351.26,
      "text": "Out your little head you got to get the parent book out, bubble out the head that could be dangerous and I don't know what to do with it. I'm C's crisis runner knows mice and Danny comes walking by what happened and she says I would boob a baby and I said she plowed in a wall she's okay. I said she goes what's the problem I said the stuff the junk coming out of her nose she Timmy goes oh you're such a wimp and Timmy she just grabs it like that this this toxic waste now she's walking down the hall berating me you're such a wimp I'm sick and tired of it was still connected to her nose."
    },
    {
      "start": 1352.09,
      "end": 1358.01,
      "text": "Oh."
    },
    {
      "start": 1355.33,
      "end": 1384.16,
      "text": "And she says to me Tammy says I bought you handkerchiefs who uses handkerchiefs? I don't see the attraction of blowing your nose and then stuffing it right back in your pocket if you want to hang on to this stuff leaving in its natural environment and my father had handkerchiefs and this is probably what and our family as a young boy if I was crying and my nose was running and my father was walking over with a ball picture oh I bet that's not up as fast as I could."
    },
    {
      "start": 1382.45,
      "end": 1402.04,
      "text": "I'm all right dad look does it come out come here boy let me wipe that face no not the handkerchief dad please not the handkerchief then you hear them open it run come here boy."
    },
    {
      "start": 1399.33,
      "end": 1409.07,
      "text": "Music."
    },
    {
      "start": 1404.93,
      "end": 1425.73,
      "text": "Oh my goodness my eyebrows I don't have any eyebrows for an entire school year my mother drew my eyebrows in every morning for an entire semester I was surprised every morning."
    },
    {
      "start": 1420.35,
      "end": 1425.73,
      "text": "[Laughter]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1428.43,
      "end": 1441.52,
      "text": "You tell yourself as a parent when they get older it'll be better that's what you tell yourself teenagers."
    },
    {
      "start": 1436.36,
      "end": 1452.76,
      "text": "I believe teenagers are God's revenge on mankind I really do. I think I think one day the good Lord was looking down over his creation and said let's see how they like it to create someone of their own image who denies their existence."
    },
    {
      "start": 1454.81,
      "end": 1475.4,
      "text": "Because I have read the Bible more than once cover to cover and it never mentions how old the devil was when he rejected God's authority if I'm guessing."
    },
    {
      "start": 1470.3,
      "end": 1482.71,
      "text": "An age, I'm saying 16, double got his driver's license, drove to Georgia, that's all I know."
    },
    {
      "start": 1485.03,
      "end": 1501.74,
      "text": "One heck of a fiddle player, buddy. You can't argue with a sixteen-year-old; you can't."
    },
    {
      "start": 1493.91,
      "end": 1514.91,
      "text": "They have a three-word vocabulary."
    },
    {
      "start": 1501.74,
      "end": 1527.84,
      "text": "That's it: \"Is\" the word? \"You're gonna pass your history test\" is another word. \"You can't spend the night at your friend's house, you flunked a history test,\" and then that word, \"Whatever, whatever,\" drove me insane."
    },
    {
      "start": 1525.23,
      "end": 1537.74,
      "text": "It affected my nerves because when it would come out of their mouths, I'd immediately point him right in the eye: \"Whatever.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 1537.74,
      "end": 1556.77,
      "text": "And gets the little angels ready for therapy. They're going, \"This is America, your kid's gonna lay on a couch at some point in his life and blame you for everything. You might as well give him reasons going in; don't stress about it; they're gonna do it.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 1553.38,
      "end": 1578.66,
      "text": "I told my kids when they learn to write, \"When you perceive an injustice in our home, do us all a favor: write it down and date, and I'll initial it.\" Boys, I'll come well. When you're middle-aged, my memory is not so good. We could breeze through the therapeutic process together. I'll just hand that book to the therapist, all that stuff with my initials next to it, I did, and I'll be at the golf course."
    },
    {
      "start": 1580.74,
      "end": 1605.15,
      "text": "I don't get it where we're at as a nation. We got 40-year-old men calling up their 65-year-old fathers and yelling at them at 2:00 in the morning and blaming them for all the grief in their life. It may be true; may have had a rotten father, but at this point in life, what do you expect them to do for you? I got my answer ready. I'm waiting for one of my sons to call me up at 2:00 in the morning: \"You ruin my life!\" All I'm gonna say is, \"Whatever.\""
    },
    {
      "start": 1612.01,
      "end": 1649.12,
      "text": "What other answer is due over, you know? \"Well, we didn't mean to ruin your life. Hey, why don't you move back home, me and your mama? Eurasia, we're a lot calmer since our strokes, don't you know? We can't remember when bedtime is; we can't remember anything. You can move home and help us find our shoes in the morning; that'll be fun. We can't find anything around here: remote controls, car keys. It's like a scavenger hunt every day. You sure do sound like a nice young man. What was your name again?\""
    },
    {
      "start": 1643.3,
      "end": 1651.67,
      "text": "[Laughter]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1649.12,
      "end": 1657.52,
      "text": "It's puberty, something happens when a child hits puberty."
    },
    {
      "start": 1656.38,
      "end": 1665.68,
      "text": "My oldest son went through puberty at 11 years old, not making it up."
    },
    {
      "start": 1663.73,
      "end": 1687.88,
      "text": "It took him a half an hour to get through it, but it was a terrible half hour."
    },
    {
      "start": 1685.48,
      "end": 1760.06,
      "text": "I'm not kidding, I called home, he's 11, he picks up the phone, 'Who are you? What's going on with you?'"
    },
    {
      "start": 1758.11,
      "end": 1774.48,
      "text": "I called home the next night, that same boy picked up my telephone, 'Hey, who are you? Put my wife on the phone and get out of my house!'"
    },
    {
      "start": 1775.4,
      "end": 1805.35,
      "text": "And they're all different."
    },
    {
      "start": 1802.11,
      "end": 1807.39,
      "text": "[Music]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1805.35,
      "end": 1813.72,
      "text": "I had the pleasure of growing up in America before the lawyers took it over and ruined it on us."
    },
    {
      "start": 1809.07,
      "end": 1816.42,
      "text": "Yeah, in my day, if the kid fell off the monkey bars and..."
    },
    {
      "start": 1815.13,
      "end": 1824.34,
      "text": "Chipped the bone in his arm, that was tragic, but it was funny to the rest of us certainly wasn't reasons to take the monkey bars off the playgrounds."
    },
    {
      "start": 1822.57,
      "end": 1828.36,
      "text": "We all did dumb things, that's how you learn not to do dumb things."
    },
    {
      "start": 1827.13,
      "end": 1830.79,
      "text": "CS Lewis said suffering was God's megaphone."
    },
    {
      "start": 1828.36,
      "end": 1834.48,
      "text": "That's right, you do dumb things, it hurts, and then you learn not to do it, but we're almost painted verses and we give you an example."
    },
    {
      "start": 1832.74,
      "end": 1869.04,
      "text": "When I was 12, someone told me get a ball jar canning jar, find some dry ice, put it in the jar, put the lid on it, so I said what's gonna happen, they said it's gonna blow up, and I said cool, where do I get dry ice, I said and they said the ice cream man, so one day I heard the ice cream man coming down my street, I run out with one of my mother's candy jars and I asked you got any dry ice, he said what you gonna do with it, and someone put it in his jar or put the lid on it and it's gonna explode, ice cream man says oh here's your dry ice, that's the America I grew up."
    },
    {
      "start": 1865.58,
      "end": 1869.04,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 1869.34,
      "end": 1904.01,
      "text": "Yes, and of course that night my mother was at our kitchen table picking shards of glass out of my forehead and my father came walking and how that happened, somebody told me you put rice in a ball jar it'll blow up, so knowing that you were just staring at that jar waiting for it to boil right up in your face, yep, what am I raising a [__], could see why you'd think that I never did it again cuz that had been really dumb, that's how you learn."
    },
    {
      "start": 1899.99,
      "end": 1929.41,
      "text": "My nephew's coming by this poor kid's eleven years old, I look at him where is he going, my sister see that rollerblading, I thought he was gonna disarm a nuclear device, poor kid look like the Michelin Man, foam rubber plastic everywhere, she says I don't want him to get hurt, I said hurt he could take a semi at 80 miles an hour, that up falling on concrete is supposed to hurt, see that your incentive to learn to stay upright on the rollerblades."
    },
    {
      "start": 1929.65,
      "end": 1954.82,
      "text": "They've ruined everything playgrounds, I took my granddaughter to a playground, what happened to playgrounds, the slide is five feet high made out of plastic, she would go for inches and stop for inches and stop for inches and stuff, it's not a slide it's a scope, we papa we papa, clapping, what did we have, we had a six."
    },
    {
      "start": 1951.25,
      "end": 1977.49,
      "text": "Story high solid steel structure Bob Mitchell, aye Mitchell, I within a temperature of about 285 degrees, you lose two layers of skin on the way down, another layer when you hit the ground like a flat rock on a pond, come back picking gravel out of your thighs, yeah."
    },
    {
      "start": 1972.8,
      "end": 1995.9,
      "text": "Now it's we pop, oh I wanted to shove her down the slide, did I want to shove her so she know what at the exhilarating feeling of sliding and I felt six iPhones on my back."
    },
    {
      "start": 1984.21,
      "end": 2018.85,
      "text": "Go ahead old man, we dare you, it's nuts car seats, I'm not against car seats, I'm just telling you I'm tired of strapping my granddaughter and like a NASCAR driver to drive two miles to get a Diet Coke from the mini-mart, she's 54 pounds, I'm gonna get a hernia hauling her and out of the city, what age can you take them out now, five, seven, 18, here's your high school diploma, you get to ride home like a big boy today."
    },
    {
      "start": 2019.47,
      "end": 2023.77,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 2021.51,
      "end": 2045.37,
      "text": "Parsi we don't even have seatbelts, I walk the backseat of my mother's car for four years, she'd be driving, I'd just be walking the back seats, I decide sure every now and then she'd hit the brakes, I'd fly up into the front, she tossed me back like a trout, what are you doing up here, is it bad I got pulled over by the police?"
    },
    {
      "start": 2043.45,
      "end": 2064.21,
      "text": "I'm not making this up, I get pulled over by the police because it was sunny day and my granddaughter was in the back seat with sunshine through the window and the policeman said I'm not gonna write you up this time but you need to have a sunshade to protect you from the Sun, I go what are you kidding, I almost got arrest, I said are you kidding me, this is a joke right?"
    },
    {
      "start": 2062.08,
      "end": 2071.92,
      "text": "When did the Sun Shine become this evil thing since we didn't have Sun so screen was when I was a kid?"
    },
    {
      "start": 2068.53,
      "end": 2085.64,
      "text": "Dirt, that's what it was, dirt and why because we would eat dirt and he would get all over our face to protect us from the Sun and then we'd wash it down with water from a garden hose and then I take a bath put on my expensive pajamas and go to bed."
    },
    {
      "start": 2083.04,
      "end": 2088.84,
      "text": "[Music]"
    },
    {
      "start": 2085.64,
      "end": 2088.84,
      "text": "And look how I turned out."
    },
    {
      "start": 2095.12,
      "end": 2108.12,
      "text": "The only kids we had worried about the Sun were the threadheads the gingers, you'd hear them sizzling like like bacon and right field out there you're rusty you might want to get in you know and."
    },
    {
      "start": 2106.71,
      "end": 2112.98,
      "text": "The kid run off to feel like a leper, pieces of them falling off."
    },
    {
      "start": 2110.51,
      "end": 2116.84,
      "text": "We still have put sunscreen on; we gave him a snow cone rubber, I knew be fine."
    },
    {
      "start": 2124.82,
      "end": 2129.93,
      "text": "I live in a small town."
    },
    {
      "start": 2127.26,
      "end": 2139.89,
      "text": "I love small towns; we have, uh, we got our first traffic light about four years ago, there were nine accidents the first week the light was installed."
    },
    {
      "start": 2137.93,
      "end": 2147.53,
      "text": "That's funny now, but apparently the people who live there their whole lives couldn't figure out what the floating red dot in the sky was, just driving along, that's new."
    },
    {
      "start": 2150.58,
      "end": 2159.31,
      "text": "I love small; I do, we moved there from Arizona, the only reason we live where we live is because there were no homeowners associations."
    },
    {
      "start": 2156.37,
      "end": 2169.92,
      "text": "When we lived in Arizona, we had a homeowner's association, and this may shock you, and we've spent about 35 minutes together, but it turns out comedy boy here has a little trouble with authority."
    },
    {
      "start": 2173.05,
      "end": 2178.93,
      "text": "Me and the local Gladys Kravitz didn't get along too well."
    },
    {
      "start": 2176.74,
      "end": 2186.46,
      "text": "They were always write me up garbage can lids her off, garage door was opened by rocks, I didn't have a want it was Arizona so I had rocks and they were messy because my kids would run through them."
    },
    {
      "start": 2185.08,
      "end": 2191.95,
      "text": "Oh, your ax a little messy, didn't know those are the ones that fell out of my head from moving here."
    },
    {
      "start": 2190.3,
      "end": 2196.86,
      "text": "The day we sold the house, we were driving out back, I told my wife I put the car in park, I said I'll be right back, and I went through the rocks."
    },
    {
      "start": 2198.73,
      "end": 2207.99,
      "text": "Terry says what are you doing, I says I'm just setting up a meeting between Gladys and the new owners because I'm a people person."
    },
    {
      "start": 2208.56,
      "end": 2220.12,
      "text": "So we moved to Tennessee, I told my wife I don't care where we live, just know Homer so that's the only rule I have; you can pick wherever we want to live, no homeowners."
    },
    {
      "start": 2217.18,
      "end": 2247.17,
      "text": "So we're driving around our little town and we it was before GPS and everything so we didn't have directions to this house we were looking for, so we figured we'll ask somebody for directions, so we're driving down the street we see a family of four, I'm the cop, I'm on their front lawn in the middle of an activity, I got to tell you I've been around, I didn't seen before; all four of them were in the process of burning the family couch, I'm the frontal, I fell out of the car, I said thank you Lord Jesus, you have brought me to the."
    },
    {
      "start": 2245.17,
      "end": 2256.87,
      "text": "Promised land, there's no homeowners association, Tammy says, is not even a conscious around here."
    },
    {
      "start": 2255.07,
      "end": 2266.13,
      "text": "Look at those people, it's like they've never seen fire all four, and we're just staring at the flames coming off, so I rolled my spare tire up to when I said fire wheel."
    },
    {
      "start": 2272.36,
      "end": 2326.73,
      "text": "The problem when you live in a small town, there's not a lot to do, so we were eating dinner one night and my oldest son was bored and he was just whiny and it was just really whiny, I said what is your problem, he goes there's nothing to do around this stupid town on the way we moved here, I said you know you're right, we too, there's nothing to do, so let's play a game, he said what kind of game, I said I'm honest and my fingers when I snap those fingers what I want you to do young man is transport yourself anywhere on this globe you want to be doing whatever your heart desires with remember your heart desires to be with this is your dream day with the person you dream to be with doing what you dream to do, I want you to dream and dream big, I want to know the dream is keeping you awake in my dreams are the engine that will drive you through this life, prayer will be the fuel that will drive those dreams, I'm gonna trust me man you stop dreaming you stop praying, you're gonna dry up bitter up and you're gonna die long before your heart ever stops beating, so when I snap my fingers young man I want you to tell me where you're at what are you doing who are you with, so I snap my fingers in my 12 year old son goes oh I'm at the mall and Bellevue playing video games with Brandon, what that's the big dream keeping you awake at night, are you kidding me?"
    },
    {
      "start": 2323.52,
      "end": 2365.43,
      "text": "How about horseback riding in Spain with JLo, yeah Tami hit me in the face with a gob of mashed potatoes, that's a dream keeping you awake at night, no man you asking me I'm on the living-room couch with you watching Law & Order baby, you guys have been great, god bless you, thank you."
    },
    {
      "start": 2359.92,
      "end": 2367.49,
      "text": "[Applause]"
    },
    {
      "start": 2365.43,
      "end": 2367.49,
      "text": "You?"
    }
  ]
}
```

## 75. Plik: `mobile\src\hooks\use-color-scheme.web.ts`

```typescript
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useColorScheme as useRNColorScheme } from 'react-native';

/**
 * To support static rendering, this value needs to be re-calculated on the client side for web
 */
export function useColorScheme() {
  const [hasHydrated, setHasHydrated] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    setHasHydrated(true);
  }, []);

  const colorScheme = useRNColorScheme();

  if (hasHydrated) {
    return colorScheme;
  }

  return 'light';
}
```

---

## 76. Plik: `mobile\src\hooks\use-theme.ts`

```typescript
/**
 * Learn more about light and dark modes:
 * https://docs.expo.dev/guides/color-schemes/
 */

import { Colors } from '@/constants/theme';
import { useColorScheme } from '@/hooks/use-color-scheme';

export function useTheme() {
  const scheme = useColorScheme();
  const theme = scheme === 'unspecified' ? 'light' : scheme;

  return Colors[theme];
}
```
